title: eriogonum cyclosepalum var. gillespiei
intro: Eriogonum cyclosepalum var. gillespiei, also known as Gillespie's buckwheat, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the buckwheat family that is native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Gillespie's buckwheat include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and provide antioxidant properties. Gillespie's buckwheat can be prepared for herbal use through infusions or decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves for its skin-healing properties.
